program_name: Environmental Engineering for the Green Transition

academic_requirements.summary: Applicants need a minimum three-year bachelor's degree, programme-specific curricular prerequisites, Teaching Committee review of the transcript and CV, preliminary online evaluation for international qualifications, and English B2.
academic_requirements.prior_degree_or_diploma: Minimum three-year bachelor's degree (or higher). International applicants must first request the preliminary evaluation of their qualification on apply.unive.it.
academic_requirements.subject_requirements: Applicants in Civil and Environmental Engineering or Industrial Engineering generally satisfy the curricular prerequisites. Applicants from other fields must have at least 60 ECTS or equivalent, including at least 21 ECTS in Mathematics, Physics, Computer Science, and Statistics, at least 16 ECTS in Civil and Environmental Engineering, plus coherent coursework in Earth and Environmental Sciences and Industrial and Environmental Engineering.
academic_requirements.gpa_or_country_requirements: For international applications, the minimum required CGPA varies by country and ranges from 70% to 77%. On the Italian-side admission page, applicants with L-7 or L-9 or equivalent and a final grade of at least 80/110 are generally considered adequately prepared.
academic_requirements.committee_or_pre_evaluation: The Welcome Unit verifies eligibility during the preliminary evaluation, while the programme's Teaching Committee evaluates the undergraduate transcript, curriculum vitae, and personal preparation.

accepted_proofs: A recognised English certification from an officially recognised certifying body in Ca' Foscari's recognised-certifications list.
A certificate from Ca' Foscari CLA or from a university language school included in Ca' Foscari's recognised-certifications list.
Documentary proof that the applicant falls within an official exemption category.
exemption_rules: Completed higher-education studies or at least one year of college in a country where English is the medium of instruction.
Obtained a high-school or university qualification after a course of study taught entirely in English.
Completed at least one full year of high-school exchange in English.
Holds one of the listed language-degree exemptions with a three-year English study path.
language_requirements.assessment_or_upload_stage: Applicants may apply before obtaining the certificate, but they must provide recognised proof of English to complete enrolment. Ca' Foscari does not conduct an English test as part of the application procedure.

rounds: Applications for 2026/2027 open from 17 December 2025 on apply.unive.it. The portal's EU/equivalent round table is nationality-routed and the detailed table is linked externally, so the accessible official pages under the allowed domains expose only the overall master's final application deadline of 17 June 2026 [uncertain].
application_deadline_eu.enrolment_deadline: Enrolment from 1 July 2026 to 30 September 2026; additional enrolment period from 1 October 2026 to 18 December 2026 with a EUR 50 surcharge.
application_deadline_eu.notes: The apply.unive.it admission-rounds article for EU/equivalent applicants gives 17 June 2026 as the most important deadline for all master's degrees. The programme admission page gives the enrolment window, but the full per-round EU table is not reproduced on the allowed official pages [uncertain].

rounds: The detailed 2026/2027 study-visa admission-round table is not reproduced on the allowed official pages; the official portal instead states that applications are closed [uncertain].
application_deadline_non_eu.visa_or_pre_enrolment_deadline: Accepted or conditionally accepted applicants who require a visa must complete Universitaly pre-enrolment by 30 September 2026.
application_deadline_non_eu.current_status: Closed for non-EU applicants requiring a study visa.
application_deadline_non_eu.notes: The apply.unive.it admission-rounds article dated 4 September 2025 states that it is no longer possible for non-EU applicants requiring a visa to apply for bachelor's or master's degrees in 2026/2027. The underlying per-round cut-off dates are not reproduced on the allowed official pages [uncertain].

required_documents.notes: European applicants may use an ID card. Applications missing a mandatory identification document are rejected.

required_documents.document_name: Valid residence permit or renewal/first-request receipt
required_documents.notes: Required for non-European applicants already residing in Italy or in the Schengen Area.

required_documents.document_name: Transcript of records for all undergraduate or bachelor's years of study, with official translation if needed
required_documents.notes: If the applicant is still attending the final year, a provisional transcript is still mandatory.
Used by the Teaching Committee to evaluate curricular coherence and personal preparation.
A dedicated motivation letter for this master's programme must be added to the application.

required_documents.document_name: Bachelor's degree final diploma, with official translation if needed
required_documents.notes: If a provisional document was used at application stage, the final diploma must be uploaded before the end of the enrolment period.

required_documents.document_name: Statement of Comparability by CIMEA, or Declaration of Value, or ARDI Statement of Correspondance with CIMEA Statement of Verification, or Diploma Supplement
required_documents.notes: For master's enrolment, one of these qualification-recognition documents is required; the Diploma Supplement is accepted only if it follows the international model and is issued by an institution in the EHEA.

required_documents.document_name: Recognised English B2 certificate or proof of exemption
required_documents.notes: Required to complete enrolment in this English-taught programme.

required_documents.document_name: Letter of enrolment eligibility and Universitaly pre-enrolment application
required_documents.notes: Non-EU applicants requiring a visa must first receive Ca' Foscari's eligibility letter and then complete pre-enrolment on the Universitaly portal.
Once the visa is issued, it must be uploaded in the relevant task on apply.unive.it to finalize enrolment.

entry_exam_or_test.test_name: No standard entry test; entry requirements evaluation only
entry_exam_or_test.applies_to: All applicants, through evaluation of curricular requirements, transcript, CV, and personal preparation.
entry_exam_or_test.dates: Three entry-requirements-evaluation sessions are published for 2026: 7 April to 4 June, 6 July to 20 August, and 1 October to 17 November, with results on 25 June, 10 September, and 4 December 2026 respectively.
entry_exam_or_test.scoring_or_threshold: There is no standard test score. The programme page states that graduates in L-7 or L-9 or equivalent with at least 80/110 are generally considered adequately prepared.

source_quotes.quote: Environmental Engineering for the Green Transition
minimum required CGPA ranges between 70% and 77%
A minimum B2-level knowledge of English is mandatory
must request a preliminary online evaluation
A motivation letter must be added to your application.
it is no longer possible for non-EU applicants requiring a visa to apply

uncertainty_notes: The detailed EU and non-EU admission-round tables for 2026/2027 are routed by nationality on apply.unive.it and linked from the official portal to external Google Drive files, which were excluded by the source policy.
For EU or equivalent applicants, the official apply portal exposes the overall master's final application deadline of 17 June 2026, while the programme page separately provides the enrolment window.

For non-EU applicants requiring a visa, the official apply portal clearly states that 2026/2027 applications are closed and the Universitaly pre-enrolment deadline is 30 September 2026 for accepted applicants.
